channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. Coming up at 9 a.m. on Monday, MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs will overturn decades of trade policy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is taking a blowtorch to the rules that have governed world trade for decades. The “reciprocal’’ tariffs that he announced Thursday are likely to create chaos for global businesses and conflict with America’s allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams’ corruption charges won’t be dropped soon: Attorney
item.description: Seven officials have resigned since the order from the Justice Department to drop the charges against Mayor Adams.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: LA Mayor Bass concedes Africa trip was 'absolutely' a mistake amid botched wildfire response
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass says she's working to restore confidence among city residents after she arrived late to the Palisades Fire response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge delays unions' request to block DOGE access to agency information
item.description: A federal judge has indefinitely delayed a ruling on a request from employee unions to block billionaire Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing internal system data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy will kick off his bid for Ohio governor in Cincinnati on Feb. 24
item.description: Republican Vivek Ramaswamy will launch his bid for Ohio governor on Feb. 24 in his native Cincinnati, with ensuing events in the Columbus, Toledo and Cleveland areas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump calls for withholding federal money from schools and colleges that require COVID vaccines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools, colleges and states that require students to be immunized against COVID-19 may be at risk of losing federal money under a White House order signed Friday by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Around 14 percent of Minnesotans have filed their 2024 taxes so far
item.description: Two months out from the tax filing deadline, the Minnesota Department of Revenue is already beginning to see a stream of return filings. The state is encouraging people to file electronically and pick the direct deposit option if they’re looking to get their returns quickly.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order to block federal money for schools, universities with COVID vaccine mandates
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Friday barring schools that still have coronavirus vaccine mandates from receiving federal funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell tests the strengths and limits of his power opposing a trio of Trump's Cabinet nominees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No longer in charge, Sen. Mitch McConnell has been speaking his mind, the long-serving GOP leader rejecting President Donald Trump’s more high-profile Cabinet nominees — alone at times, among the Republicans, casting his no votes.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge sets a 5-day deadline for the Trump administration to start lifting its USAID funding freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has ordered President Donald Trump's administration to temporarily lift a funding freeze that has shut down U.S. humanitarian aid and development work around the world, and he has set a five-day deadline for the administration to prove it's complying.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — There was guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi, including F-35 stealth fighter jets, to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speedy cross-border work by Minnesota’s MMIR Office credited with quick return of First Nations woman to Canada
item.description: Feb. 14 has dual significance. It’s Valentine’s Day, but also a day of remembrance to honor missing and murdered Indigenous relatives. It’s also a day to celebrate triumphs.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: EPA chief says he will seek return of $20 billion in clean-energy grants awarded by Biden
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a major reversal, the new head of the Environmental Protection Agency said he will try to rescind $20 billion in grants awarded by the Biden administration for climate and clean-energy projects.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Egg price bill advances at Nevada Legislature; AB171 could increase supply in 30 days
item.description: A bill aimed at reducing the skyrocketing price of eggs in Nevada advanced on Monday, but the legislative process will take some time even if there's no opposition.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida could ban these chemical additives in foods by 2028 if bill passes
item.description: Florida could ban chemical additives in foods by 2028 under a new bill that was filed on Monday by a Southwest Florida lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5
